# moodle-cli 0.2.5

## Summary

This patch release fixes `okta-auth` integration for split `uv tool` installs, so `moodle-cli` can trigger automatic Okta login even when `okta-auth-cli` is installed in a separate tool environment.

## Highlights

### okta-auth CLI fallback

`moodle-cli` already supported automatic Okta login through the `okta_auth` Python adapter when both packages were available in the same environment.

That did not work for the common setup below:

- `uv tool install moodle-cli`
- `uv tool install okta-auth-cli`

In `0.2.5`, if the adapter module is not importable, `moodle-cli` now falls back to the external `okta` / `okta-auth` executable.

It will:

1. read stored cookies through `okta cookies <url> --json`
2. validate the returned `MoodleSession`
3. trigger `okta login <url> --json` when needed
4. retry cookie lookup after login

### Better install model

Users no longer need to co-install `okta-auth-cli` into the `moodle-cli` tool environment just to make automatic login work.

The default separate-tool install now works as expected.

## User-visible changes

- `moodle` can reuse `okta-auth-cli` sessions even when `okta-auth-cli` is installed separately
- automatic login now works through the external `okta` executable when the Python adapter is unavailable
- README now documents `okta-auth-cli` as an optional companion install

## Validation

Validated with:

- `uv run pytest tests/test_auth.py`
- `uv run pytest tests/test_cli_e2e.py -q`
- `uv build`
